## Coding

Built **TheLog** from scratch over one long session.

Started with a React + Vite app (`daily-log-viewer`) and rebuilt it twice — first attempt had a game/sci-fi aesthetic which got scrapped. Second version is a real wooden bookcase: warm brown wood textures via CSS gradients, books with fabric grain and cream page edges, gold tassels on the year summary books, and a lock modal with 12 hex bolts and fake combination dials. The secret unlock is the bottom-right bolt (`br2`).

Merged the existing `daily-log` markdown journal repo into the viewer so everything lives in one place (`the-log`). Set up the project structure:
- `logs/` — markdown entries by year/month/day
- `src/` — React frontend (Bookcase → Book → LockModal → BookViewer)
- `scripts/build-logs.js` — generates `src/data/logs.json` from the markdown files

Productive day on the computer. Training in the evening — deadlift cluster day with solid numbers. Hit 195 for 3, then jumped to 205 for 3 at RPE 8 which is strong for clusters; the blood rush on the first set made it hard to gauge and nearly threw me off mentally on the second, but got it done. Bench on a commercial rack felt awkward technically but the weight moved well — 130 for 3 at RPE 7 on an unfamiliar setup is a good sign. Finished with hip thrusts and lat pulldowns as planned.
